mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate over the preceding three months raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations during sustained FEDFUNDS increase periods, or Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated and the unemployment rate is simultaneously rising, restrictive monetary policy amplifies labor market weakness, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days that exceed the negative effect of either variable in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE changes fails to add significant explanatory power for 20-day forward returns (F-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal produces positive returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in housing starts predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter when monetary policy is accommodative, as low financing costs amplify construction multipliers and downstream consumer spending.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 3%, positive momentum in HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production indicates immediate supply-side weakening that precedes negative earnings revisions and drags S&P 500 returns lower over the next trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO contracts month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand that translates into corporate revenue beats and expanding valuations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: RSAFS 3-month momentum fails to produce a rank correlation greater than 0.05 with 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment combined with rising payroll employment confirms a broad-based labor market recovery that reduces recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over twenty trading days; neither series alone captures the full breadth of improvement.
failure_condition: Joint signal of falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS does not outperform the better of the two single-series signals by at least 2 percentage points in directional accuracy for 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is slowing while the federal funds rate remains above five percent, restrictive monetary policy into a decelerating economy predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as recession risk gets priced in.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 2.0%
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS above 5% and GDPC1 growth below 2% occurs but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances.
